message:
fix(dependency-eradication): exclude runtime_async path from FP-08/FP-09 tokio-runtime forbidden patterns (ft-g43fq deprecation window)

Adds `runtime_async` to the `exclude_paths` list for two of the
standard forbidden-pattern rules in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/dependency_eradication.rs::standard_forbidden_patterns`:

- FP-08-tokio-runtime  (severity = Critical, runtime = Tokio)
- FP-09-tokio-runtime  (severity = Critical, runtime = Tokio)

Prior exclude list (per pattern):
    ["vendored/", "runtime_compat"]

New exclude list (per pattern):
    ["vendored/", "runtime_async", "runtime_compat"]

Why:
- `runtime_async` is the canonical runtime-surface module after the
  ft-g43fq rename. Existing internal call sites still reach the
  surface via the deprecated `runtime_compat` substring during the
  one-release deprecation window — that substring already lived in
  the exclude list. With the dual-runtime seams collapsed
  (9e1bd63b under ft-xbnl0.2.5), some imports now go directly
  through `crate::runtime_async::*` instead of the alias path,
  and the FP-08/FP-09 scanner was flagging those as critical
  tokio-runtime violations even though they're the canonical
  surface that is allowed.
- The fix is exclusion only — no FP-08/FP-09 detection logic
  changes, no change to severity, no change to `allow_in_tests`,
  and no other patterns are touched. The `runtime_compat`
  substring stays in the list because the deprecation window
  isn't over yet.

What this is NOT:
- Not a permission grant for tokio runtime usage anywhere outside
  the explicit exclude paths. Critical FP-08/FP-09 violations
  outside `vendored/`, `runtime_async`, and `runtime_compat`
  still fire as before.
- Not a removal of `runtime_compat`. That removal is tracked
  separately as ft-y378j.4 and depends on completing the alias
  deprecation window.

Documentation:
- Inline comment added next to FP-08's exclude_paths spelling
  out the reason (`runtime_async` is the canonical runtime
  surface; `runtime_compat` is the temporary deprecation alias;
  ft-y378j.4 removes the alias).
- The same exclude list is applied symmetrically to FP-09 so
  the two rules don't drift; no comment is duplicated on
  FP-09 because the rationale is identical.

Scope:
- Surgical: the two `exclude_paths` vectors gain one element
  each. No other field, struct, function, or pattern is altered.

Co-author note: change authored by a parallel agent in this
session; committed verbatim per the
"treat parallel-agent changes as your own" rule from AGENTS.md.

files.patch:
@@ -155,15 +155,27 @@ pub fn standard_forbidden_patterns() -> Vec<ForbiddenPattern> {
             runtime: ForbiddenRuntime::Tokio,
             severity: ViolationSeverity::Critical,
             allow_in_tests: false,
-            exclude_paths: vec!["vendored/".into(), "runtime_compat".into()],
+            // runtime_async is the canonical runtime surface (ft-g43fq).
+            // The deprecated alias substring stays in the list during the
+            // one-release deprecation window so a stray import via the
+            // alias path still passes the scanner. ft-y378j.4 removes it.
+            exclude_paths: vec![
+                "vendored/".into(),
+                "runtime_async".into(),
+                "runtime_compat".into(),
+            ],
         },
         ForbiddenPattern {
             pattern_id: "FP-09-tokio-runtime".into(),
             pattern: "tokio::runtime".into(),
             runtime: ForbiddenRuntime::Tokio,
             severity: ViolationSeverity::Critical,
             allow_in_tests: false,
-            exclude_paths: vec!["vendored/".into(), "runtime_compat".into()],
+            exclude_paths: vec![
+                "vendored/".into(),
+                "runtime_async".into(),
+                "runtime_compat".into(),
+            ],
         },
     ]
 }
